4 · Findings by Account

ASHISH DESAI weak
acct_002 · BANDHAN BANK LIMITED · Account Number 98036543420467 · Score 161
Rank #1

This account is suspicious due to the presence of strong patterns and distinct patterns, indicating potential money laundering or illicit activities. The transactions between acct_002 and acct_004 show rare amount mirror matches and narration similarity matches. The large transfer of ₹400000.25 from acct_002 to acct_004 on 2025-05-19 is also notable.

  • Round Trip Detection strong — On an unspecified date, transactions between acct_002 and acct_004 showed a rare amount mirror match and narration similarity match, suggesting potential money laundering or illicit activities.
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — On 2025-05-19, a large transfer of ₹400000.25 from acct_002 to acct_004 occurred with no prior transactions between the accounts.
  • Cross Statement Links weak — A matched flow link was found based on a rare amount mirror match corroboration path with a confidence score of 0.65, involving a debit of ₹400000.25 from acct_002 to acct_004. (+1 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 1185 nodes and 3034 directed transaction edges was constructed, indicating a complex network of financial transactions across accounts acct_001, acct_002, acct_003, and acct_004.
  • Money Trail Tracing weak — An initial credit of ₹354676.31 was made to acct_002 on 2025-05-21, and the amount was then traced through 79 subsequent debit transactions, including ATM cash withdrawals and UPI transactions to various merchants.

acct_004 · Bank of India · Account Number 4258497598930953 · Score 160
Rank #2

This account is suspicious due to a large credit transaction of £400000.25 and subsequent debit transactions. The money behaviour suggests potential money laundering or other illicit activities. The transactions between acct_002 and acct_004 have raised several red flags.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit transaction of £400000.25 was traced into 11 subsequent debit transactions, with the entire amount allocated and no remaining balance, indicating a potential money laundering scheme.
  • Round Trip Detection strong — The round-trip detection pattern suggests that transactions between acct_002 and acct_004 may be related to money laundering or illicit activities due to a rare amount mirror match and narration similarity match.
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of £400000.25 from acct_002 to acct_004 on 2025-05-19 is notable due to the lack of prior transactions between the two accounts, indicating a potential first contact large transfer.
  • Cross Statement Links weak — A matched flow link was found based on a rare amount mirror match corroboration path with a confidence score of 0.65, indicating a potential cross-statement link between debit account acct_002 and credit account acct_004. (+1 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 1185 nodes and 3034 directed transaction edges was constructed, indicating a complex network of financial transactions across multiple accounts, potentially revealing patterns of money laundering.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
